statement: Organizers of events should not be able to make a request for a vaccination certificate upon entry.
The Netherlands should not make its exit from the European Union (EU).
During the upcoming New Year's Eve, it should not be given permission to set off decorative fireworks again.
There should be a no additional tax on making a purchase of meat.
The government should not effect the abolition of the ban on face-covering clothing.
The national government, rather than provinces and municipalities, should not make a decision where new residential areas are built.
The government should not make reductions in the VAT on cultural activities to 5 percent.
The Netherlands should not effect the construction of a new nuclear power plant.
The Dutch government should not make an apology for the historical slave trade.
Teachers in elementary schools should not have earnings as much as teachers in secondary schools.
The Netherlands should not effect the introduction of an additional air tax for short-haul flights.
Asylum seekers with provisional residence permits should not effect the completion of integration before they are given rental housing.
Both the purchase and sale of soft drugs by coffee shops should not be given legal authorization.
An increase in minimum wages should still automatically give rise to an increase in welfare benefits.
New residential areas should not have a proportion of at least 40 percent green space.
The Netherlands should not effect the acceptance of more refugees than it currently does.
Limiting rights and freedoms is not necessary to take up the fight against organized crime.
It should not be easier for companies to effect the dismissal of workers.
The state should not take measures to effect the redistribution of wealth from the rich to the poor.
The government should not effect an increase in spending on the public health system even if this means increasing taxes.
Working hours should not be given a reduction without reducing workers' wages.
Governments should not make as few interventions in the economy as possible.
Taxes on fossil fuels should not be raised to effect the financing of the Green Transition.
The future Spanish government should not effect an increase in irrigated agricultural areas by means of large water transfers.
To better defend Spain's interests in Europe we should not effect the recovery of more sovereignty.
The Spanish government should not give a boost to the strengthening of NATO in Europe.
The best way to solve the conflict in Catalonia is that its citizens cannot cast a vote on their future in a referendum.
Negotiating with the independentistas does not effect a weakening of the State.
The right to self-determination should not be given recognition by the Constitution.
The policies of linguistic immersion in the language of the bilingual Autonomous Communities do not have a detrimental effect on Spanish.
It is not necessary to effect the repeal of the Law of Democratic Memory passed during this legislature.
Immigrants should not make payments for their own health services.
No general speed limit is to take effect across all highways.
Germany should not effect an increase in its defense spending.
Young people aged 16 and over should not be granted permission to vote in federal elections.
The ability of landlords to increase housing rents should not be given stricter restrictions by law.
All employed persons are not to be given necessary requirement for being insured in the statutory pension scheme.
The traditional family of father, mother and children is not to be given a stronger boost than other living arrangements.
Donations from companies to political parties should not be given permission.
The Nord Stream 2 Baltic Sea pipeline, which transports gas from Russia to Germany, should not be given permission to go into operation as planned.
Female civil servants should not be given permission to wear headscarves while on duty.
The federal government should not make provision for more financial support for projects to combat anti-Semitism.
Chinese companies should be given permission to receive contracts for the expansion of the communications infrastructure in Germany.
The state should not effect the collection of church tax for religious communities.
The controlled sale of cannabis should not be given general approval.
Germany should not make its exit from the European Union.
No facial recognition software should be given permission to be used for video surveillance in public places.
Organic agriculture should not be given a stronger boost than conventional agriculture.
Islamic associations should not be given recognition by the state as religious communities.
The statutory minimum wage should not be given an increase to at least 12 euros by 2022 at the latest.
Air traffic should not be given heavier taxation.
Companies should not make decision for themselves whether to allow their employees to work from home.
Social transfers should not be given an increase to reduce the effects of inflation on citizens.
The state should not give funding for private visits to specialists if the waiting time at a public facility exceeds three months.
The state should not make provision for a free nursery place for every child.
Schools should not have more freedom to make a choice of the content covered in the curriculum.
The state should not effect the construction of low-rent apartments for rent.
Taxes should not be given an increase for top earners.
Not all entrepreneurs should make payment of the same amount of health premium regardless of income.
Poland should not effect the adoption of the migrant relocation solutions adopted by the European Union.
The share of defense spending in Poland's GDP should not be given a further increase.
The powers of local governments should not be given an increase at the expense of the central government.
Public media funding from the state budget should not be given restrictions.
The powers of the secret services to track the activities of citizens on the Internet should not be given restrictions.
The EU's rule of law mechanism does not make threats against Hungary's sovereignty.
Hungarian foreign policy should not be given guidance solely by Hungarian economic interests.
Hungary should not make a decision by referendum whether to remain part of the EU.
The age of compulsory education should not be given an increase back to 18.
Education spending should not be given an increase to at least the OECD average of 5.2 per cent (GDP).
Teachers' salaries should not be given a twofold increase.
The change in the university model (reorganisation into a trust) has not been given a reduction by changing the university model (reorganisation into a trust).
Not only men and women should be given permission to marry.
The state should not take targeted measures to give a boost to equal participation of fathers and mothers in child-rearing.
The Hungarian government should not give ratification to the Istanbul Convention, which combats violence against women and domestic violence.
The conclusion of favourable gas supply contracts with Russia is not an effective way of making reductions in rationing.
Not to give an increase in the contribution of the wealthier to the public purse (abolition of the one-band tax).
A price freeze on some basic foodstuffs (e.g. chicken tail, milk) is not the right way to take up the fight against inflation.
The current three-month unemployment benefit should not be given an extension.
The family tax allowance is no better way of giving support to families than increasing the family allowance.
It is not right that the Education Office, rather than parents and kindergarten teachers, should make a decision on the school entry maturity of children from the age of six.
The use of medical cannabis should not be given legal authorization in Hungary.
Polluting companies should not be given heavier taxation.
In larger cities, car traffic should not be given restrictions through various measures (P+R parking, construction of cycle paths, improvement of public transport).
The redevelopment of urban green spaces (e.g. the Liget project in Budapest) does not have a need for a broad social dialogue.
European economic integration has not gone too far: member states should not effect the regaining of more autonomy.
Citizens should not be given a guarantee of freedom of choice in end-of-life matters (euthanasia).
Recreational use of marijuana/cannabis should not be given permission.
A law is not needed to effect the prevention of companies' relocating their production abroad.
Businesses should not be allowed to effect the dismissal of employees more easily.
The introduction of a single income tax rate ("flat tax") would not have benefits for the Italian economy.
Beach concessions to private individuals should not be given time limits.
The use of nuclear power plants for the purpose of producing energy should not be given a boost.
Italy should not effect the construction of more incinerators/thermal power plants.
Drilling is not necessary to make discoveries of more energy resources.
Italy should not effect the cessation of the sending arms and war material to the Ukrainian government.
The federal government should not make an allocation of more funding for health insurance premium subsidies.
As part of the reform of the BVG (occupational pension plan), pensions are not to be given a reduction (lowering the minimum conversion rate from 6.8% to 6%).
Paid parental leave should not be given an increase beyond today's 14 weeks of maternity leave and two weeks of paternity leave.
The federal government should not make provision for more financial support for public housing construction.
Insured persons should not make a greater contribution to health care costs (e.g., increase the minimum deductible).
The Federal Council's ability to restrict private and economic life in the event of a pandemic should not be given stricter restrictions.
The federal government should not be given the authority to make a determination on the hospital offering (national hospital planning with regard to locations and range of services).
The federal government should not effect the raising of the requirements for the high school.
More qualified workers from non-EU/EFTA countries should not be given permission to work in Switzerland (increase third-country quota).
Cannabis use should not be given legal authorization.
Doctors should not be given permission to administer direct active euthanasia.
Married couples are not to be given separate taxation (individual taxation).
The differences between cantons with high and low financial capacity should not be given a further reduction through fiscal equalization.
Private households should not be free to make a choice of their electricity supplier (complete liberalization of the electricity market).
Switzerland should not have a ban on the registration of new passenger cars with internal combustion engines starting in 2035.
It's fair that environmental and landscape protection rules are not being relaxed to give permission for the development of renewable energy.
The construction of new nuclear power plants should not be given permission again.
The state should not give a guarantee of a comprehensive public service offering also in rural regions.
There should not be a popular initiative aims to make reductions in television and radio fees (CHF 200 per household, exemption for businesses).
The voting age should not be given a reduction to 16.
The army's target number of soldiers should not have an expansion to at least 120,000.
The Swiss Armed Forces should not have an expansion of their cooperation with NATO.

The Federal Council should not be allowed to give authorization to other states to re-export Swiss weapons in cases of a war of aggression in violation of international law (e.g., the attack on Ukraine).
Switzerland should not effect the termination of the Schengen agreement with the EU and should not effect the reintroduction of more security checks directly at the border.
Companies should not be obliged to give assurances that their subsidiaries and suppliers operating abroad comply with social and environmental standards.
Switzerland should not effect the termination of the Bilateral Agreements with the EU and seek a free trade agreement without the free movement of persons.
Switzerland should not make a return to a strict interpretation of neutrality (renounce economic sanctions to a large extent).
